DEMURRER TO 5TH AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-02-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: MICHAEL BUTLER, 415.756.7746, PLAINTIFF IN PRO PER.
SCOTT KANTER, ESQ., 408.861.6505, FOR DEFENDANT LAUREEN PARAGUYA.

ULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling REGARDING DEFENDANT LAUREEN PARAGUYA'S DEMURRER TO 5TH AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant Paraguya's demurrer is sustained, without leave to amend. Mr. Butler's claim of fraud is barred by the statute of limitations. (CCP 340.5.) CCP 340.5 applies to a cause of action for deceit based on the physician's false representations or fraudulent concealment of the nature and extent of the injury. (Weinstock v. Eissler (2002) 224 Cal.App.2d 212, 227.) "Notwithstanding a defendant's continuing efforts to conceal, if plaintiff discovers the claim independently, the limitations period commences." (Sanchez v. South Hoover Hospital (1976) 18 Cal.3d 93, 99.)
Further, Plaintiff has alleged that the last overt act of conspiracy that was May 2, 2010 when the California Department of Public Health sending a letter to Mr. Butler. The 1 year Statute of Limitations would still have expired May 1, 2011, before Plaintiff filed the original complaint Jude 29, 2011. Also, the Court cannot construe the 90 day letter Plaintiff allegedly sent to Defendants to toll the statute of limitations by 90 days per CCP 364 because the letter is undated, and Defendants did not have actual notice of the letter. (See Hanooka v. Pivko (1994) 22 Cal.App.4th 1553, 1560.) Order signed in open Court. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.

DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-10-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael E. Butler, plaintiff in pro per.
Scott Kanter, Esq., 408.861.6500, for defendant Laureen Paraguya, M.D.
Marcell P. Neri, Esq., 415.288.9800, for defendants Sutter Health, Sutter West Bay Hospitals (DBA California Pacific Medical Center) and Alan Pont, M.D.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling with modification: DEFENDANT ALLAN PONT M.D, SUTTER HEALTH, SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS (DBA CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER) DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained, with leave to amend. Mr. Butler's claims are barred by the statute of limitations. (CCP 340.5.) CCP 340.5 applies to a cause of action for deceit based on the physician's false representations or fraudulent concealment of the nature and extent of the injury. (Weinstock v. Eissler (2002) 224 Cal.App.2d 212, 227.) "Notwithstanding a defendant's continuing efforts to conceal, if plaintiff discovers the claim independently, the limitations period commences." (Sanchez v. South Hoover Hospital (1976) 18 Cal.3d 93, 99.) Mr. Butler admits that he discovered the fraud in August 2008. (Fourth Am. Complaint, p. 4:4-14, 7:9-12.) Further, Mr. Butler alleges that the last overt act that occurred on May 2, 2011 was perpetrated when the California Department of Public Health sending a letter to Mr. Butler. (Fourth Am. Complaint, p. 9:27 - 10:3.) None of the defendants were involved in sending this letter. Lastly, Mr. Butler admits that any claim for patient dumping is barred by the statute of limitations. (Id. at p. 14:19-21.) None of the defendants were involved in sending this letter.
Order signed in open Court. Judge: A. James Robertson II, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael E. Butler, plaintiff in pro per.
Scott Kanter, Esq., 408.861.6500, for defendant Laureen Paraguya, M.D.
Marcell P. Neri, Esq., 415.288.9800, for defendants Sutter Health, Sutter West Bay Hospitals (DBA California Pacific Medical Center) and Alan Pont, M.D.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANTS ALLAN PONT M.D, SUTTER HEALTH, SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS (DBA CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER) Motion For Monetary Sanctions is denied. Sutter West errs in moving for sanctions against Mr. Butler per CCP 128.5 because the statute applies only to complaints or cross-complaints filed prior to December 31, 1994. (CCP 128.5(b)(1).) APPEARANCES - MICHAEL E. BUTLER, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER;
ATTY. SCOTT R. KANTER OF THE LAW OFFICES OF HINSHAW, MARSH & HINSHAW, TEL. NO. (408) 861-6500 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANT LAUREEN PARAGUYA'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS Overruled. The plaintiff has alleged a discovery date for the alleged fraud that renders his fraud claim not time-barred. Plaintiff has also alleged an explanation why that date differs from the discovery date alleged in his prior complaint. That explanation, if true - and the court must assume that it is true for purposes of this demurrer - is sufficient to avoid the prior date from being binding on the plaintiff. THE REQUEST TO FILE A 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077 =(302/HEK)
